claim: Pilot synthetic-lethality screen on 196 biologically-curated gene pairs (5 categories) using the v15 composed detector framework. The methodology is technically sound — Session-26 Invariants 1-3 all pass, and Categories B (same-pathway) and C (random) stay safely below the spec's halt thresholds (0.0 % each vs 15 % / 10 % caps). But the signal is weak: only 1 of 50 paralog pairs (2.0 %) flags as synthetic-lethal, which does not meaningfully separate from the 0/41 random-pair baseline. The single hit (JCVISYN3A_0876 x JCVISYN3A_0878) is biologically interpretable — paralogous unannotated genes at ESM-2 cosine 0.996 whose joint knockout silences 18 amino-acid transport rules (ALAt2r, ARGt2r, ASNt2r and 15 others). 0876 is Breuer-Nonessential and 0878 is Breuer-Quasiessential, consistent with a redundant-paralog pair where losing both starves amino-acid uptake. Decision: NARROW_SCOPE — do not run the full ~105k-pair screen at the current sampling density. The paralog category was dominated by pairs of essential-family paralogs (38 % of A-pairs had at least one already-essential gene, 42 % the other), so most paralog pairs cannot be synthetic-lethal by definition. A focused next pilot on paralogs that BOTH single knockouts call non-essential, plus shared metabolic neighborhood, would have the right denominator.
source_detail: Reproducible via scripts/synthlet_pilot_pairs.py (pair selection) + scripts/run_synthlet_pilot.py (execution). Output: outputs/synthlet/pilot_predictions.csv (196 rows). Input: outputs/synthlet/pilot_pairs.csv (196 pairs across 5 categories). Wall: 1680 s (28 min) on 4 workers, well within the 90 min cap. Config matches v15 exactly (scale 0.05, t_end 0.5 s, dt 0.05 s, seed 42, Rust on, iMB155 patches on, min_wt_events 20). Invariants 1+2 verified by cell_sim/tests/test_pairwise_harness.py (14 tests pass); Invariant 3 (v15 reproducibility on 20-gene sample) verified by scripts/run_synthlet_pilot.py --verify-only with 20/20 matches written to outputs/synthlet/viability_check.txt.

synth_lethal_denominator_across_pilot.note: Synthetic-lethality is only defined when both single knockouts are non-essential. Most A-paralog pairs picked by ESM-2 cosine sit on essential gene families (ribosomal, RNA pol, translation factors), so the denominator was small; the 1/50 hit rate is closer to 1/20 against the eligible denominator.

top_predictions.mechanism: joint knockout silences 18 amino-acid transport catalysis rules (ALAt2r, ARGt2r, ASNt2r + 15 more); single knockouts silence subsets but do not cross the detector's threshold; consistent with paralogous amino-acid transporters where one substitutes for the other under each single knockout.
top_predictions.wet_lab_followup: double knockout in Syn3A; measure growth at 36 C across three independent biological replicates. If 0876 alone grows fine, 0878 alone shows mild defect (per Breuer Quasiessential), but 0876+0878 fails to grow, the synthetic-lethal prediction is confirmed.

value.rationale: Paralog synth-lethality rate 2.0 % does not meaningfully separate from the 0.0 % random baseline at this sampling density. The methodology works (Invariants 1-3 pass, halt criteria pass, single hit is biologically interpretable) but the signal-to-noise ratio is too low to justify a 105k-pair screen. The right next step is a narrower pilot: ~500 pairs filtered to (a) both single-knockouts non-essential at v15, (b) shared metabolic-neighborhood metabolite, (c) ESM-2 cosine in the 0.85-0.95 band (the very-high-cosine top of the distribution overlaps too heavily with self-similar paralogs of essentials). If a focused screen produces >= 10 % synth-lethality rate vs <= 2 % random control, the full screen becomes proportionate.

caveats: Pilot screen on 196 of approximately 105,000 possible pairs (C(458, 2) on the full Syn3A gene set). Per-category synthetic-lethality rates are pilot-scale point estimates with wide confidence intervals — 1/50 = 2.0 % is consistent with anything from 0.05 % to 11 % at 95 % confidence (Wilson interval).

No wet-lab validation. The single synthetic-lethal call (JCVISYN3A_0876 x JCVISYN3A_0878) is a simulator-derived hypothesis. Its biological credibility comes from the mechanism trace (18 amino-acid transport rules silenced jointly) plus the Breuer-2019 single-knockout calls (Nonessential + Quasiessential), but the actual double-knockout phenotype has not been measured.

Category B (same-pathway sequential pairs) is a deliberate negative control. Its 0.0 % rate is reassuring but partly trivial: 86 % of B-pairs have one gene already essential, so the synth-lethal denominator excludes most of them. Category B's role as a negative control would be more informative if filtered to pairs where both singles are non-essential.

Category C (random non-essential pairs) showed 0 / 0 / 0 across single-A, single-B, and pair essentiality — every pair was non-essential at all three measurement points. This is the cleanest baseline rate but also tells us the simulator does not flag random non-essential joint knockouts as essential, period.

Category D (transporter-substrate) and E (manual) sat at 100 % / 80 % pair_essential rates respectively because most pairs in those categories had at least one Breuer-essential gene; their 0 % synth-lethal rates reflect the eligibility issue, not a methodology gap.

The simulator's known limitations (Layer 5 not implemented, ~75 % knowledge-based detector weight at v15, 0.5 s biological-time window) carry over to pairwise predictions. A pair flagged synth-lethal here may reflect the same detector-noise patterns that produce v15's 3 stubborn false positives at single-knockout level.

If the user wants to proceed without the narrow pilot, the alternative is to publish this as a NEGATIVE result for the methodology: 'event-driven Gillespie + composed detector at 0.5 s simulation window does not produce paralog-vs-random synthetic-lethality separation at the pilot density tested'. That negative is itself informative for what works and what doesn't in mechanism-aware double-knockout prediction.

Compute: 28 min wall on 4 workers for 349 tasks (153 singles + 196 pairs). Per-task average 19 s, well above the v15 single-knockout 6.6 s/gene because pair simulations spend more wall time in detector evaluation (the composed detector runs detect_for_gene twice per pair, once per locus, on the joint trajectory).
